title: Baby Steps
description: A classic SQL injection vulnerability lurks in this login form. Can you bypass authentication and retrieve the admin's password?
hint: Think about what happens when you add a single quote to the username field.

title: Cookie Monster
description: The admin panel checks your cookie for authentication. But the cookie looks suspiciously like base64...
hint: Decode the cookie, modify the 'role' field, re-encode it.

title: XSS Playground
description: The search feature reflects your input directly. Steal the admin's session cookie using a stored XSS payload.
hint: Try injecting a <script> tag in the comment section.

title: ROT-What?
description: We intercepted this message: 'UC{ebg_guvegrra_vf_abg_rapelcgvba}'. The sender seemed to think this was secure.
hint: Caesar cipher with a twist. The flag format is HP{...}

title: RSA Warmup
description: We captured an RSA encrypted message. The public key uses a suspiciously small modulus. Can you factor it?
hint: Use a factorization database like FactorDB for small moduli.

title: Magic Bytes
description: This image file seems corrupted, or is it? Sometimes files hide other files. Dig deeper.
hint: Check the file's magic bytes. Then try binwalk or steghide.

title: Memory Dump
description: We captured a RAM dump from a compromised machine. The password was in memory somewhere...
hint: Use Volatility Framework. Look at process memory and registry hives.

title: Overflow Me
description: A classic buffer overflow vulnerability in a 32-bit binary. No stack canaries, no ASLR. Pop a shell!
hint: Find the offset to EIP using a cyclic pattern, then redirect to your shellcode.

title: Format String
description: The server passes user input directly to printf(). Can you use this to leak the flag from the stack?
hint: Use %p%p%p to start leaking stack values. Look for printable ASCII strings.

title: CrackMe 1
description: A simple crackme binary. Enter the correct password to get the flag. No source code provided.
hint: Open it in Ghidra and look for string comparisons in the main function.

title: Packed Malware
description: This binary is packed with UPX. Unpack it, then reverse engineer the decryption routine to find the flag.
hint: Use 'upx -d' to unpack, then analyze the XOR decryption loop in IDA.

title: Shodan Recon
description: Find the flag hidden in the HTTP response headers of a specific misconfigured server on Shodan. Search for servers exposing 'HP-FLAG' header.

title: Google Dork Hunt
description: A company accidentally exposed a sensitive file on their web server. Use Google dorks to find it. The company domain is: target-corp.hackerplus.io

title: Misc: The Beginning
description: Welcome to HackerPlus CTF! Your first flag is hiding in plain sight. Check the source code of this challenge page.
hint: View page source. Right-click → View Page Source.

title: Netcat Basics
description: Connect to our challenge server using netcat and answer a series of simple math questions to get the flag. Host: nc.hackerplus.io Port: 4444
